runners.name: Wield
runners.spotlight: Juddmonte colt who was well held in sole 2yo start but the race has worked out well; fared better with third at Chelmsford (7f, AW) on reappearance; respected, with another step forward on the cards.
runners.comment: Well held in sole 2yo start but the race has worked out nicely; ran well on reappearance

runners.name: Powder Monkey
runners.spotlight: Both starts on AW, finishing second to useful sort in 7f event at Wolverhampton and a fading third over 1m at Southwell; possibilities with this drop back in trip looking favourable.
runners.comment: Both starts on AW, second to useful sort in 7f event and a fading third over 1m

runners.name: Cerro Blanco
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett half-brother to 1m3f Flat (RPR 75) and 2m hurdle winner Secret Force, out of 7f/1m scorer (including Listed; 107); powerful stable; interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: Colt by Wootton Bassett out of Listed scorer; powerful stable; interesting newcomer

runners.name: Makerstoun
runners.spotlight: 500,000euros yearling; by Night Of Thunder and first foal of French Listed-placed 1m 2yo winner (RPR 101); in line with the other newcomers, could be interesting.
runners.comment: 500,000euros yearling; by Night Of Thunder; could be interesting

runners.name: Big Snoopy
runners.spotlight: 300,000gns yearling; by Siyouni out of 7f/1m winner (including French 1,000 Guineas; RPR 107); one of three debutants in the field; the betting should be instructive.
runners.comment: 300,000gns yearling; by Siyouni out of French 1,000 Guineas winner; check the betting

runners.name: Galba
runners.spotlight: Front-running third of seven in autumn maiden (6.5f, soft) that wasn't particularly deep by usual Newbury standards; however, may still show improvement this year.
runners.comment: Front-running third in autumn maiden at Newbury; may be capable of improvement

runners.name: Kimbara
runners.spotlight: Brings the worst form, having finished only fourth of five at Newcastle (AW) where he was admittedly the sole newcomer in the field and perhaps found 6f on the sharp side.
runners.comment: Perhaps needed the experience and found 6f on the sharp side in Newcastle event

runners.name: Alpine Culture
runners.spotlight: Finished fourth of eight, best of the newcomers, in 7f AW maiden at Southwell four months ago; runner-up has franked the form by scoring twice since; open to progress.
runners.comment: Fourth of eight, best of the newcomers, in 7f AW maiden at Southwell; open to progress

races.race_name: Gabriela Rose London British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Adaay Of Scarlett
runners.spotlight: February foal; 110,000euros yearling; first foal; by Mehmas out of Group-placed dual 6f winner (RPR 105), half-sister to winners Morte Point (6f/7f including 2yo; 97), Study Up (6f) and three other winning sprinters (two on debut); yard's first 2yo runner this term was easy to back but won on debut; interesting contender.
runners.comment: 110,000euros yearling; speedy pedigree; yard's first 2yo runner was easy to back but won

runners.name: Innichen
runners.spotlight: £65,000 yearling; out of French Listed-placed 5f winner as 2yo; second favourite for Bath debut (5f, good) but badly hampered when going for a gap, then again later, beaten nearly 6l; much better can be expected today.
runners.comment: Second favourite for Bath debut but hampered twice; much better can be expected today

runners.name: Shadow King
runners.spotlight: March foal; sixth foal; Dark Angel half-brother to winners Involvement (7f-1m2f including 2yo/UAE; RPR 108) and Dubai Crystal (1m2f; 87); dam 1m 2yo/1m2f Listed winner (third in Oaks; 111); yard won with first 2yo runner this year; needs a market check but bred for longer trips.
runners.comment: Out of 1m2f Listed winner; pedigree suggests 5f will be on the sharp side
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Anna Belardo
runners.spotlight: January foal; £5,000 yearling; first foal; by Belardo out of 5f 2yo winner (including on debut; RPR 85), half-sister to winners Eddie's Boy (5f-6f, including 2yo French Group 3) and Masaru (useful 6f-1m including 2yo); yard had a beginner run well on AW recently and this one is not discounted.
runners.comment: Early foal; £5,000 yearling; related to several precocious types; not discounted

runners.name: Rocket Boots
runners.spotlight: March foal; second foal; by Too Darn Hot out of unraced half-sister to winners Father Of Jazz (useful 9.4f-1m2f Flat/2m hurdle), Save A Forest (1m4f Listed) and Liam Swagger (1m4f Flat/Listed hurdle); surprising if he's sharp enough for this test given his pedigree.
runners.comment: Out of half-sister to useful winners on Flat and over hurdles; hard to fancy on 5f debut

races.race_name: Federation Of Bloodstock Agents EBF Conditions Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Talk Of New York
runners.spotlight: Odds-on for a couple of 1m contests, showing a good turn of foot to win at Kempton (AW) then clear third at Meydan (Listed) where he did reasonably well having pulled hard in the early stages; may settle better dropped in trip with hood applied; holds a 2,000 Guineas entry (prominent in that market) and remains a promising colt.
runners.comment: May settle better dropped in trip with hood applied, and he remains a promising colt

runners.name: Maximized
runners.spotlight: Brings useful 6f form that includes a success in the Woodcote as a 2yo and two placed efforts, since a gelding operation, at Meydan this year; a reasonably solid contender over this new trip, albeit William Buick prefers stablemate Talk Of New York.
runners.comment: A reasonably solid contender over this new trip, albeit William Buick deserts him

runners.name: Shaatir
runners.spotlight: Good fourth in Chantilly Listed contest (6.5f, AW) last month, despite having been absent since midfield effort in the 2025 Coventry; chance of landing this useful race partly depends on how well he responds to first-time headgear; steps up a tad further in trip.
runners.comment: Chance of landing this race partly depends on how well he responds to headgear

runners.name: Richie's Rocket
runners.spotlight: Showed big promise in the autumn, finishing third over C&D then scoring impressively at Lingfield (7f, AW); beaten favourite in Listed event at latter track on reappearance but ran well in terms of form, despite a tardy start; open to further progress and remains of major interest.
runners.comment: Beaten favourite in Listed grade on reappearance but ran well; remains of interest

runners.name: Lost Signal
runners.spotlight: Progressive last summer and the form has substance; finished third to the subsequent Dewhurst winner then justifying favouritism twice, the final performance at Ffos Las (7.4f, good); probably has a better chance than bare ratings suggest.
runners.comment: Progressive last summer and the form has substance; won at Ffos Las the final time

runners.name: Sierra Sands
runners.spotlight: Probably didn't need to improve to win Catterick maiden (7f, good) on last appearance but he has raced only three times and remains open to progress; switches to handicap level with Ryan Moore booked; one to consider.
runners.comment: Catterick winner who remains open to progress; Ryan Moore booked; handicap debut

runners.name: Try Storm Cat
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-6 for Charlie Johnston, the success over 6f (has form at 1m); failed to beat a rival when last seen and sold for 11,000gns since; perhaps best watched unless the market suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival on final start for Charlie Johnston; sold for 11,000gns since

runners.name: Kody B
runners.spotlight: Lacked consistency last term; registered a 5l win in Pontefract maiden (6f, soft) the final time but it remains to be seen whether he can find the necessary further progress to defy a 7lb rise in his mark; tongue-tie fitted.
runners.comment: By no means certain to find the necessary further progress to follow up Pontefract win

runners.name: Hardy's Hero
runners.spotlight: Gelded since never landing a blow in Newbury nursery (7f) on final 2yo start when bidding for a hat-trick after maiden/novice wins over 6f; represents major stable and may still have more to offer; not written off.
runners.comment: Gelded since failing to complete a hat-trick; represents major yard and not written off

runners.name: High Approval
runners.spotlight: 400,000euros breeze-up buy who failed to live up to expectations over 6f last year, for all that he was placed three times; chance of belated progress depends on the effects of new trip and switch to handicap level.
runners.comment: Chance of belated progress depends on the effect of new trip and switch to a handicap

runners.name: Startled
runners.spotlight: Has consistent form since being gelded and reappearance effort (6f, AW) suggests this new trip is worth exploring, in keeping with his pedigree; scored comfortably in sole attempt in a turf handicap; solid claims.
runners.comment: Has consistent form since being gelded; won his sole attempt in a turf handicap; solid

runners.name: Daretobedifferent
runners.spotlight: Quite an exposed maiden sprinter last term and was well behind Cashbox on final outing (AW); combination of new trip and another headgear switch needs to have a significant effect.
runners.comment: Quite an exposed maiden sprinter last term; new trip needs to help

runners.name: Harswell Angel
runners.spotlight: Gained his 2yo success at 6f but reappearance effort over that trip suggests he'll be suited by this step back up in distance; balance of form suggests he'll probably come up short in this field, however.
runners.comment: Worth another crack at 7f but balance of form suggests he'll come up short

runners.name: Crazee Icon
runners.spotlight: Threatening to open her account, having registered a couple of second-place finishes since switched to handicap level, latest at Goodwood (7f, good) six months ago; possibilities provided she can progress this year.
runners.comment: Couple of second-place finishes since handicapping; threatening to open her account

runners.name: Starryfield
runners.spotlight: Handicap debutant who has consistent sprinting form (placed three times) on turf/AW and could go well provided he stays this longer trip; mixed messages in his pedigree on that front; gelded since last run.
runners.comment: Consistent sprinting form; could go well, provided he stays this new trip

runners.name: Cashbox
runners.spotlight: Two from two since handicapping and gained the latest success over C&D (good to firm); gelded during his subsequent six-month absence; commands respect up another 4lb, with further progress plausible.
runners.comment: 2-2 since handicapping and gained the latest success over C&D; gelded since; respected

runners.name: Senor Campanaro
runners.spotlight: Doesn't seem totally straightforward but he showed the benefits of a gelding operation at Newcastle (7f, AW) on final 2yo start and may build on that success; raised 4lb but Ashley Lewis takes off 5lb.
runners.comment: Showed the benefits of a gelding operation when last seen; may build on that win

runners.name: Stellar Vision
runners.spotlight: Twice-raced colt who looks the type to improve further and do well in handicaps; eye-catching third at Newmarket (6f, good) in October then won well at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) in January; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Eye-catching third at Newmarket then won well on AW; type to improve further; interesting

runners.name: Regal Envoy
runners.spotlight: Multiple wins include this race (good) in 2025, on sole C&D start, then won two of his next three starts; his form died down after that which leaves him well treated again, 5lb lower than his latest win; good claims but he did seem better for his reappearance when coming up trumps last April.
runners.comment: Won this in 2025, then 2 out of next 3; 5lb lower than the latest win; lacks recent run

runners.name: Rosario
runners.spotlight: Strong traveller who can be slowly away; first handicap win when beating Existent at Goodwood (5f, good) last May; treading water during the summer but he did well when a close third of 21 from a tough draw in the Portland (extended 5f, good) in September and a subsequent Ascot defeat by a well-treated rival was another solid run given the race didn't go his way; claims.
runners.comment: Strong handicap form in the Portland and over 5f at Ascot in the autumn; claims

runners.name: Existent
runners.spotlight: Close call in a Group 3 here in 2022 and deserves so much better than his very modest turf strike-rate, given how many fine races he continues to run in defeat, such as when going down narrowly to both Rosario and Regal Envoy on different 5f runs (good and good to firm) last May; some cold comfort that he won on AW in January but tough to support for the win.
runners.comment: Just one turf win to show for some sterling close calls over 5f against smart rivals

runners.name: Sturlasson
runners.spotlight: Below best in Ireland in the first half of 2025 but in good heart for this trainer in the autumn, winning three over 5f on AW and turf; has inevitably gone back up the weights but he ran an excellent second, despite some interference, when runner-up in the 3yo Dash at Epsom in 2024 off this mark and that was strong 5f form; respected.
runners.comment: Recaptured form for new yard; strong 5f turf form off this mark in 2024; considered

runners.name: Tan Rapido
runners.spotlight: An improved spell over 5f last July included an AW win and a close second in a Racing League race at Yarmouth (soft); unable to sustain that level after but he hasn't had many chances in 5f handicaps and may well do better this year, if not today.
runners.comment: Improved 5f form last July; not sustain that level after; may yet do better at this trip

runners.name: Jakajaro
runners.spotlight: Turf strike-rate of 1-27 is offputting but he broke bread with talented sprinters in 2025 and ran with great credit when third of 27 in the Stewards' Cup at Goodwood (6f, good to soft) in August and the Portland at Doncaster (extended 5f, good; similar weights chance as the third Rosario) in September; has won after a break; form claims.
runners.comment: Just 1-27 on turf but ran with great credit in two of the best sprint handicaps in 2025

runners.name: Dyonisos
runners.spotlight: All three wins at about 6f as 3yo in France; has run to a useful level on the AW (6f) and at Meydan (5f, good) for this yard but now needs to show he can translate the latter form back to Britain.
runners.comment: All 3 wins at 6f in France; good 3rd at Meydan latest; has to transfer it back to Britain

runners.name: We Never Stop
runners.spotlight: Often leads; first two wins on turf (6f/5f; good to firm) but better on AW and it's a long time since he showed much on grass; slow to find form on sand over the winter but he's dropped a few pounds now back at 5f; one to keep an eye on for yard that has won two of the last nine runnings.
runners.comment: Better on AW; long time since he's shown much on turf but yard won 2 of last 9 runnings

runners.name: Major Neigh Sayer
runners.spotlight: Comfortably held in heavy-ground Listed event in France when last seen; won well at Newmarket (7f, good) the time before, taking record in domestic races to 321; possibly remains open to further progress.
runners.comment: Won well at Newmarket in last domestic attempt; possibly remains open to further progress

runners.name: Tiger Power
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced colt who prevailed at Southwell (1m, AW) in sole outing this year, adding to his 2yo success at Haydock (only turf attempt); hit with an 8lb rise in his mark but may improve further; handicap debut.
runners.comment: Record of 2-3 includes a success in sole turf attempt; may improve further

runners.name: We're Goosers
runners.spotlight: Solid record over 7f last autumn comprised a couple of second-places, including at Newmarket, then odds-on success at Newcastle (AW); may well progress further and do well in handicaps; warrants respect.
runners.comment: Solid record last autumn included a Newmarket effort; may well progress further; respected

runners.name: Akho Mezzna
runners.spotlight: Improved slightly on some consistent efforts to win at Salisbury (7f, good) on final 2yo start; gelded since; mark has been nudged up only 2lb for this handicap debut; may take another step forward.
runners.comment: Gelded since Salisbury win; may take anther step forward on handicap debut

runners.name: Darn Hot Gallop
runners.spotlight: Related to Enable; two from two, namely a couple of 7f AW wins in November, latest in ready fashion at Newcastle; this filly is evidently held in good regard, being a 1,000 Guineas entry; promising contender.
runners.comment: Two from two, namely AW wins; holds a 1,000 Guineas entry; promising contender

runners.name: Arbaawy
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden last term and his form rather levelled off after a couple of good seconds; market support would be encouraging but otherwise needs improvement to make a winning handicap/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Six-race maiden last term; needs improvement to make a winning reappearance

runners.name: St Anton
runners.spotlight: Produced consistent form figures (322) and steadily progressive RPRs last season, running well at Salisbury (1m, good) the final time; solid handicap debutant and going one better is far from implausible.
runners.comment: Has consistent form figures and steadily progressive RPRs; solid handicap debutant

runners.name: Pandemonium
runners.spotlight: Showed his autumn form over 7f on soft/good to soft; well held in the Horris Hill when bidding to take his record to 3-3; however, gained his wins in good style and may have more to offer back down in class.
runners.comment: Well held in the Horris Hill but gained his wins in good style and may do better still

runners.name: Raging Raj
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced at Southwell (1m) when bidding for an AW hat-trick, taking his form figures since handicapping to 2112; ran well in last turf attempt but is now 17lb higher in a stronger grade.
runners.comment: Record of 2112 in handicaps but he is now 17lb higher than last turf attempt

runners.name: Luzon Heights
runners.spotlight: Record of 412 at Class 5 level since handicapping, the last two performances over 1m on AW; he's 6lb than last turf appearance and goes up in grade; needs to find further progress.
runners.comment: Sound results in Class 5 since handicapping but needs to find further progress

runners.name: Blue To Blue
runners.spotlight: Record of 3-8 last year for Karl Burke and ran well in Newmarket contest (7f, good to firm) on final start; sold for 60,000gns since; the market signals may help to ascertain whether he'll return in the same form.
runners.comment: Record of 3-8 last year for Karl Burke and ran well at Newmarket on final start

runners.name: Blue Courvoisier
runners.spotlight: Both starts in September, winning at Leicester (7f, good) then respectable second at Bath (1m, good to form); gives the impression he may need headgear or further still but is otherwise still unexposed.
runners.comment: May need headgear or further still but is otherwise still unexposed

races.race_name: Betway Bet The Responsible Way Handicap

runners.name: Mister Winston
runners.spotlight: Easily made all in small field on the other Newmarket course in August on just his second handicap start; after a 10lb rise he turned in a respectable fourth of 11 at Goodwood (also 1m) a fortnight later but was down the field here in September's Cambridgeshire (1m1f, also good to firm); has been gelded and might have more to offer this term.
runners.comment: Easy winner on July course in August; has been gelded and may do better again this term

runners.name: Great Chieftain
runners.spotlight: Good seventh of 23 here in the Cambridgeshire when second home on his side; not much went right on UAE debut at Meydan (1m, good) in January but he was a solid fifth of 16 over that C&D a fortnight later and is now back down to his last winning mark; C&D winner who looks a key player.
runners.comment: C&D winner who is back down to his last winning mark and ran well in January; key player

runners.name: Real Gain
runners.spotlight: Won a 1m1f handicap here in September 2023; didn't get going the following term but returned from a year's absence with fine third of 15 in Goodwood's Golden Mile (1m, soft) last August; weakened when favourite for the Cambridgeshire here (1m1f, good to firm) the following month and he left Richard Hughes after beating two home at Kempton (AW) in November; stable debut.
runners.comment: Left R Hughes after some lesser efforts but he can go well after an absence; stable debut

runners.name: Christian David
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner including of a good-ground 1m handicap in June 2024; first-time blinkers when doing well to win 7f AW handicap (now just 1lb higher) narrowly in December; low-key AW return in March but it was a fair effort when ninth in the Lincoln (1m, good to soft) three weeks later; fitness guaranteed.
runners.comment: Fair ninth in Lincoln; fitness guaranteed and his mark is fine too; worth a second look

runners.name: Esherann
runners.spotlight: Third attempt when cheekpieces were applied in a Naas maiden, seeing it out well to turn over a long-odds on favourite (pair clear) in June; left Dermot Weld for 95,000euros after winning Curragh handicap (also 1m, good; first-time visor) in August; up 7lb and a tongue-tie is today's aid of choice for this stable/seasonal debut; unexposed.
runners.comment: 95,000euros buy after Curragh h'cap win; tongue-tie on for yard/seasonal debut; unexposed

runners.name: Bopedro
runners.spotlight: 10yo who won this in 2023; frequently runs well in quality handicaps and he's 5lb lower than when a cracking third in the Royal Hunt Cup last June; scratched from the Lincoln when running a temperature on his intended comeback last month and he tends to improve for a run these days; also winless since 2023.
runners.comment: Won this in 2023 but no wins since July course success that August; might need this run

runners.name: Sterling Knight
runners.spotlight: Record of 8-42 on turf, the most recent win at Doncaster (7f, good to soft) last July; finished last on his AW return in March and no impact when beaten 10l in Doncaster's Spring Mile a fortnight later, but he's won over 1m and there's nothing wrong with this mark.
runners.comment: Last won in July; needs to step up on last two starts but nothing wrong with this mark

runners.name: Mythical Guest
runners.spotlight: Both wins came over 1m2f in the spring of 2024, including here; 0-18 subsequently and he's still 7lb above his last winning mark, but he goes well fresh (has won after a longer break) and finished third on his comeback in this race last year; likely to be competitive.
runners.comment: 0-18 since last win (1m2f) but has won after a longer break and was 3rd in this last year

runners.name: Tycoon
runners.spotlight: Windsor maiden winner for the Gosdens last spring; out of the frame in three 1m2f handicaps, including two for this yard, but wasn't beaten far on stable debut at York (good) in September (gelded since) when wearing cheekpieces; disappointed on AW when last seen in November and goes without headgear again but he's not fully exposed and this drop down in trip might help.
runners.comment: Yet to get going in h'caps but he's not fully exposed; drops in trip for this reappearance

runners.name: Arisaig
runners.spotlight: Talented mare although her last win was back in July 2024; started last year's campaign with solid fifth of 14 in Newbury's Spring Cup (good) and ran well on plenty of other occasions, notably when close third in Goodwood Group 2 (also 1m, good to firm) in August; creditable third in C&D Listed event the following month but has career-high mark to contend with on this return.
runners.comment: Talented mare but no wins since July 2024 and she's on a career-high mark for this return

races.race_name: CopyBet Supporting UK Horse Racing Amateur Jockeys' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Hurricane Bay
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped if judged on his two good-ground wins in cheekpieces last spring (2m7f/2m5f) but has been out of form (in various headgear) in recent months; sure to appreciate this ease in grade but needs to turn a corner; cheekpieces refitted.
runners.comment: Sure to appreciate this ease in grade but needs to turn a corner

runners.name: No But I Will
runners.spotlight: Just held on to make winning stable debut under Jessica Milly Smith at Fakenham (2m5f, soft) in December and returned from break with fairly good fourth at Wetherby (2m3f, good to soft) last month; will also be suited by today's trip-and-ground combination; no surprise if he features.
runners.comment: Won on stable debut in December and also ran well when reappearing last month; a possible
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Donald Smith

runners.name: Giovanni Change
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent in recent years and no longer the force of old but kept on well to score at Wetherby (3m, good) last month, and this six-time course winner is sure to be suited by the return to this track.
runners.comment: Inconsistent nowadays but won last month and is sure to be suited by return to this track
runners.owner: Readers & Wiggy, The 8 Amigos & J Burns

runners.name: Mt Fugi Park
runners.spotlight: Disappointed on first two starts this season but seemed to be running well in first-time blinkers (retained) before falling at Carlisle (3m, good to soft) last month; remains on same mark as for last spring's good-ground win there and has top amateur Gina Andrews booked here; might be the answer.
runners.comment: Seemed to be running well before falling last time; remains on last winning mark

runners.name: Ladronne
runners.spotlight: Veteran who has looked as good as ever for his new stable this season, winning twice (2m5f/3m1f); probably doesn't have much margin for error off current mark but reverts to chasing after a respectable hurdle run and is suited by this C&D.
runners.comment: Veteran who has looked as good as ever for his new stable this season, winning twice

runners.name: Knockanore
runners.spotlight: Generally very disappointing since winning last season's Eider Chase at Newcastle (4m1f, good to soft) by 16l but took small step back in right direction when fourth over hurdles last month (wore first-time blinkers) and is too well handicapped to rule out on this return to chasing; usual cheekpieces back on.
runners.comment: Latest hurdle run was small step back in right direction; on dangerous chase mark

races.race_name: First Past The Post At CopyBet Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Diamonds Galore
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on first three hurdling starts this season, losing out only narrowly on handicap debut at Ludlow (2m, soft; front pair clear) in February; clearly not herself when pulled up in valuable series final last month but should be fine on the forecast good ground here and will be hard to beat if back on song.
runners.comment: Not herself in valuable race last month; sets clear standard on earlier form
runners.owner: Turf Club 2024 & The Burling Family Ltd

runners.name: Chatty Girl
runners.spotlight: Dug deep to win soft-ground Newbury bumper on debut last season and has made the frame on three of her four hurdling starts this term; does not appear to be making any progress but ought to be on the premises here.
runners.comment: Bumper winner who made the frame on three of first four hurdling starts; in the mix again

runners.name: Briery Rose
runners.spotlight: Out of connections' smart jumper Briery Belle; not beaten far when fifth of 13 in Uttoxeter bumper in December 2024 but returned from long layoff with less encouraging bumper run last month; improvement required.
runners.comment: Made quite promising bumper debut in 2024 but returned from layoff with a lesser effort
runners.owner: Mrs H Plumbly, J Trafford, K Deane

runners.name: Georgia's Charm
runners.spotlight: Placed in two bumpers in 2024 and bettered inauspicious stable/hurdle debut when fifth of 12 at Ludlow (2m, good) last month; needs another step forward to play anything more than a minor role here, though.
runners.comment: Second hurdle run was much better than the first; another step forward is needed, though

runners.name: Flying Pimpernel
runners.spotlight: Placed in two of her three bumpers last year but both hurdle runs last month (1m7f/2m3f) were disappointing; still has plenty of time to get her act together over jumps but arrives here with quite a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Placed in two of her three bumpers last year but first two hurdle runs were disappointing

runners.name: Pour Tout Le Monde
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far in her first two bumpers in 2024 but finished down the field on her latest outing, and this hurdling debut comes after a 519-day absence; hood refitted.
runners.comment: Showed only minor promise in bumpers in 2024; makes hurdle debut after long absence

runners.name: Curious Mrs Fox
runners.spotlight: Lowly rated Flat maiden; seemed to take pretty well to hurdling when respectable fourth at Newcastle (2m4f, good to soft) a fortnight ago but has quite a lot to find here.
runners.comment: 0-25 on the Flat; recent hurdling debut was respectable but she has a lot to find here

races.race_name: Safer Gambling At CopyBet Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Jimmy Hurdstrom
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in two good-ground bumpers last spring and showed fair form on all four hurdling starts this season, most recently when third on handicap debut at Warwick (2m5f, good to soft) last month; leading contender back in novice company.
runners.comment: Placed in 0-120 Warwick handicap last month; major player back in novice company

runners.name: Plains Drifter
runners.spotlight: Second to subsequent Grade 2 winner Montemares over C&D (good to soft) on hurdle debut in December and also placed on both subsequent outings, latterly when back here last month; respected.
runners.comment: Second to smart rival over C&D on hurdle debut and placed again twice since; respected

runners.name: Manalishi
runners.spotlight: Second on bumper debut at Chepstow (good) last April; made jumping errors when well-held when third in Doncaster novice hurdle (2m, soft) in November but has plenty of scope for improvement if he finds some fluency on this return to better ground, and his pedigree suggests the new trip will suit.
runners.comment: Second in a bumper; made jumping errors when well-held third on hurdle debut; can improve

runners.name: Dontelimurnamepike
runners.spotlight: Showed minor promise against own age-group on last two outings, more recenty when keeping on steadily for never-dangerous fifth at Wetherby (2m, soft; ride attracted stewards' attention) in January; needs significant improvement here, though.
runners.comment: Showed minor promse against own age-group on last two starts; improvement needed here

runners.name: Sims' Law
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to stable's useful jumper Coeur Serein but struggled (albeit on soft ground) on his first two appearances (2m/2m4f).
runners.comment: With a good stable but struggled on his first two appearances

runners.name: Ebony Ebbs
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in both bumpers and when 200-1 for last month's hurdling debut (2m3f).
runners.comment: Well beaten in both bumpers and when 200-1 for last month's hurdling debut

runners.name: Lord Chamberlain
runners.spotlight: Fair maiden on the Flat who greatly advanced his form on his handicap hurdle debut when scoring easily at Hereford (2m) eight days ago; major shout under a 7lb penalty.
runners.comment: Greatly advanced form on h'cap debut with easily Hereford win; major shout under 7lb pen

runners.name: Assorda
runners.spotlight: Scored twice (at up to 2m3f) in early 2025 but she's shown little after 12 months off in a pair of handicaps at Catterick and Sedgefield for her current yard this year; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Has shown little after 12 months off in handicaps at Catterick and Sedgefield this year

runners.name: Ever Ready
runners.spotlight: Remains winless and she comes here on the back of a below-par sixth in 2m4f Southwell handicap three weeks ago; others are preferred.
runners.comment: Remains winless and comes here on back of a below-par sixth at Southwell three weeks ago

runners.name: Caramelo Rapido
runners.spotlight: Opened his account at Doncaster (2m) in February but he beat only one in 2m Hexham handicap 20 days ago; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Beat only one in 2m Hexham handicap 20 days ago; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Hans Lippershey
runners.spotlight: Fair bumper performer who has matched that form in this sphere, still not foot perfect when a good second of five in 2m Fakenham novice last month; this return to further should suit so he's very much one to consider, especially if hurdling better.
runners.comment: Good second at Fakenham latest; very much one to consider in his bid for a maiden win

runners.name: Ambassador
runners.spotlight: Back to winning ways at Cartmel (2m1f) in August but his form has nosedived since, beating just one after a lay-off at Sedgefield (2m1f) 13 days ago.
runners.comment: Won at Cartmel in August but his form has nosedived since

runners.name: Ruler Legend
runners.spotlight: Readily gained a first victory in over two years when capitalising on a reduced mark in 2m handicap here nine days ago; carries a 7lb penalty but can go well again.
runners.comment: Readily gained first win in over two years here recently; can go well under 7lb penalty

runners.name: Play Pretend
runners.spotlight: Scored at Worcester (2m) last July and posted a very good second of 11 in 2m4f handicap there when last seen out in October; this course winner has gone well fresh so must enter calculations.
runners.comment: Very good second at Worcester in October; this course winner must enter calculations

runners.name: Nitty Gritty
runners.spotlight: A fairly useful 2m4f hurdles scorer for Joseph O'Brien; recorded much his best effort for his current yard when fifth of ten  in 2m4f handicap at Wetherby on Boxing Day; not out of things if building on that after a break.
runners.comment: Best effort for current yard when fifth at Wetherby on Boxing Day; not out of things

races.race_name: Live Racing Streaming At CopyBet Handicap Chase

runners.name: Catchim
runners.spotlight: Well hell when third at Southwell (2m, good) three weeks ago but that was his first start for three months and he ran well to a point; well handicapped if judged on his two seconds in November (2m4f/2m) and has strong claims if that recent outing did him good.
runners.comment: Well handicapped on autumn placed form and ran well to a point last month, after a break

runners.name: Grain Trade
runners.spotlight: Won readily by 6l here (2m3f, good) in October and was unlucky not to follow up at Fontwell (2m2f, good to soft) soon afterwards; managed only fourth over C&D on last two appearances but current mark is workable if this race is run to suit his usual patient tactics.
runners.comment: Course winner in October; current mark is workable if this race is run to suit

runners.name: Jiair Madrik
runners.spotlight: 0-5 over fences but ran quite well when last of four finishers at Bangor (2m1f, good to soft) last month, despite racing a bit too freely; sports refitted hood here and has been given a wind op; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Ran quite well at Bangor last month and has been given a wind op since; shortlisted

runners.name: O'Grady's Hill
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced when third (well held in the end) at Taunton (2m2f, good) last month but generally disappointing for new stable this season and now 0-10 under rules.
runners.comment: Not disgraced at Taunton last month but generally disappointing for new stable this season

runners.name: Greenways
runners.spotlight: Badly out of form since the autumn but has a first-time tongue-tie fitted here and is now now 10lb lower than when winning at Uttoxeter (2m, good) last summer; well worth a glance in the betting.
runners.comment: Badly out of form since the autumn but still worth a glance in the betting

runners.name: Chateau Du Lys
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in two handicap chases this season (2m5f/2m) and now 0-8 under all codes; sports first-time cheekpieces here; 1lb out of the weights; leap of faith required to support him.
runners.comment: Pulled up in two handicap chases this season and now 0-8 under all codes

races.race_name: Market Rasen Racecourse Caravan & Touring Park Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Cuban Court
runners.spotlight: 3-4 over fences, the last two wins gained by wide margins in small fields at Doncaster (2m3f, good/good to soft); shooting up the weights but evidently in very good nick.
runners.comment: 3-4 over fences, the last two wins gained by wide margins in small fields at Doncaster

runners.name: Southoftheborder
runners.spotlight: Took nine attempts to open chasing account but the form of his win at Kempton (2m2f, good) three weeks ago was nicely boosted by the runner-up on Saturday, and this fellow is still on a good mark after a 3lb rise.
runners.comment: Beat a next-time-out winner when scoring at Kempton last month; only 3lb higher here

runners.name: Singapore Trip
runners.spotlight: Five-time hurdle winner who has returned to chasing in recent weeks and was a clearcut winner at Wetherby (1m7f, good) on his latest outing; features prominently in calculations.
runners.comment: Has returned to chasing in recent weeks and was a clearcut winner at Wetherby last time

runners.name: Icare Colombe
runners.spotlight: Not an instant success over fences this season but cheekpieces seem to have helped lately and he won by 5l off a reduced mark here (2m1f, good to soft) last month; moves up in grade today but should be fine over the longer trip and might use that recent success as a springboard.
runners.comment: Up in grade after last month's course win but further progress is possible

runners.name: Kayce Dutton
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner this season (2m5f/3m); too inconsistent to rely upon heavily, though, and was no match for winner when second at Southwell (2m4f, good) a fortnight ago; swaps cheekpieces for first-time visor; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Dual course winner this season (2m5f/3m) but took inconsistent to rely upon heavily

races.race_name: Book Summer Plate Ladies Day Today Mares' Novices' Handicap Chase (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)

runners.name: All The Glory
runners.spotlight: Has rather mixed record over fences this season but belied 40-1 odds when close third in valuable Windsor handicap (2m4f, soft) won by Bluey in January and last month's Cheltenham Festival defeat is easily excused; on good mark if on her mettle.
runners.comment: Has mixed record this season but her Windsor third in January brings her right into this

runners.name: Bluey
runners.spotlight: Below best when beaten 1-3 favourite in Wincanton Listed race in February but the ground was heavy there and she's better judged on her game win in valuable Windsor handicap (2m4f, soft) two starts ago; also suited by good ground; high on the list.
runners.comment: Beaten at short odds last time but the ground was heavy; player if judged on previous win

runners.name: She Is For Me Boys
runners.spotlight: Created pretty good impression when winning on chase/handicap debut at Kempton (2m4f, good) in November; yet to build on that effort but ran quite well when third at Warwick (2m, good to soft) last month and ought to be better suited by today's trip-and-ground combination; 1lb wrong; could have a say.
runners.comment: Yet to build upon promising chase debut in the autumn but still enters calculations

runners.name: Molto Bene
runners.spotlight: Launched chasing career with two wins in the autumn (2m4f/2m7f) and came second in two subsequent Listed races (hurdle/chase); lost out only narrowly to a next-time-out winner when back in a handicap at Leicester (2m4f, good to soft) last month and has a fighting chance here.
runners.comment: 2-4 over fences; close second to a next-time-out winner last time; has a fighting chance
runners.owner: Hot To Trot Jumping And Ffl Bloodstock

runners.name: Epinephrine
runners.spotlight: Last month's keeping-on second at Bangor (2m1f, good to soft) came in just a run-of-the-mill handicap and she's now 0-4 over fences but she's unexposed over this sort of trip and may still have a bigger run in her; unraced on ground quicker than good to soft.
runners.comment: Last month's Bangor second came in an run-of-the-mill handicap but she may yet do better

runners.name: Jasmine Bliss
runners.spotlight: Opened chasing account at second attempt, with Listed success at Huntingdon (2m4f, soft) in February, and was creditable second in Grade 2 Pendil at Kempton (2m4f, good to soft) later that month; still has potential over fences; respected on handicap return.
runners.comment: Listed winner in February and second in Grade 2 Pendil later that month
runners.owner: Noel Fehily Racing Syndicate - Jasmine B

runners.name: Lunar Discovery
runners.spotlight: Seemingly back in prime form when beating a subsequent Cheltenham winner at Chepstow (2m3f, good to soft) in December but wasn't at same level when weakening third at Stratford (2m6f, good) last month, after a break, and needs that recent run to have brought her on.
runners.comment: Back in prime form at Chepstow in December but not quite as good last month, after a break

races.race_name: CopyBet Daily Profit Boost Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Numero Ventotto
runners.spotlight: Third foal; dam bumper winner, sister to hurdle winner Touchy Feely, closely related to useful bumper/jumps winner Sure Touch, out of useful close relative of Cheltenham Gold Cup winner Long Run; well-bred filly from a top stable; interesting.
runners.comment: Out of a well-connected bumper winner and represents a top stable
runners.owner: Hot To Trot Racing & Robert Waley-Cohen

runners.name: Popalot
runners.spotlight: Out of a bumper winner; shaped with significant promise when third of five in tactical contest over C&D (good to soft) in November, and the form has worked out quite well in the meantime; one to consider.
runners.comment: Shaped with significant promise when third of five over C&D in November

runners.name: Crystal Glade
runners.spotlight: Seventh foal; half-sister to winners Rainyday Woman (Listed bumper/hurdle) and William Cody (2m4f chase); dam useful jumper; trainer is not prolific in bumpers but has done well with this filly's family.
runners.comment: Trainer is not prolific in bumpers but has done well with this filly's family

runners.name: Denis The Badger
runners.spotlight: Frontiersman gelding from an unraced dam; stable has only occasional bumper winners.
runners.comment: Third foal from an unraced dam; stable has only occasional bumper winners

runners.name: Royal March
runners.spotlight: Third foal; dam 2m4f hurdle winner, sister to a 2m4f chase winner; wily trainer doesn't have many runners nowadays but his bumper horses are always worth a look.
runners.comment: Out of a 2m4f hurdle winner; wily trainer's bumper horses are always worth a look

runners.name: Sea Lord
runners.spotlight: Third foal; brother to 1m2f/1m4f AW winner Ocean Wave (RPR 92), half-brother to 1m AW winner Ocean Manifest (83); dam Listed-placed 8.6f/1m4f AW winner (95), half-sister to dam of French 1,000 Guineas/French Oaks winner La Cressonniere; first ever National Hunt runner for respected Flat trainer.
runners.comment: Brother to a useful Flat winner; first National Hunt runner for respected Flat stable

runners.name: Middlecliffe
runners.spotlight: Has some good jumpers in his family but was well beaten at triple-digit odds on both starts last month.
runners.comment: Well beaten at triple-digit odds on both starts last month

runners.name: Bite The Bullet
runners.spotlight: Sprint bred on his dam's side; with a respected stable that generally does well in bumpers but struggled when 22-1 for debut at Huntingdon (2m, good to soft) in March and needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Stable usually does well in bumpers but this fellow struggled on last month's debut

races.race_name: St. Austell Brewery Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Laffer Curve
runners.spotlight: Has shown promise in three hurdle runs, producing her best effort on recent handicap debut at Huntingdon (2m4f, good) when third off a mark of 103; needs to find a bit with Onefournine on the figures but should be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Need to find a bit with Onefournine on the figures but should be thereabouts.

runners.name: Onefournine
runners.spotlight: Off for over a year after an Irish point win in 2024; built on hurdle debut promise when a neck second at Fontwell (2m2f, soft) in January before a disappointing run when favourite at Kempton the following month; that tempers enthusiasm but she sets the standard on earlier form and her pedigree suggest she'll be suited by the step up in trip.
runners.comment: Disappointing latest but she's up in trip and sets the standard on earlier form.

runners.name: Kaydence
runners.spotlight: Fair bumper winner who was always out the back when switched to hurdles and beaten 29l at Wincanton (1m7f, good to soft) last month; pedigree suggests she'll be well suited by this step up in distance and is one to take seriously.
runners.comment: Fair bumper performer and her pedigree suggests she'll be suited by this longer trip.

runners.name: Queens Up
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam a 2m hurdle winner; showed promise when third in December on second start in an Irish point (3m); that form has some substance with the fourth winning since; makes hurdle/stable debut but may find a couple too strong.
runners.comment: Runner-up in an Irish point in December and that form has some substance.

runners.name: Davita Blaze
runners.spotlight: Well held in her bumper and, although shaping with a bit more promise, never threatened on hurdle debut at Hereford (2m4f, good) 20 days ago; goes up in trip but she may be one for further down the road; stable looks to have a stronger contender in Onefournine.
runners.comment: Never threatened on hurdle debut and may be one for the longer term.

runners.name: Stavanger Rose
runners.spotlight: Yet to complete in four career starts, pulling up in an Irish point and again on hurdle debut in February and she has since fallen/unseated midrace on latest two starts; it's safer to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Yet to complete in four career starts and it's safer to look elsewhere.

runners.name: Dunaden Island
runners.spotlight: Won a soft-ground maiden point in January 2024 but her form dipped markedly; returned from a break with a more encouraging fourth on February's hurdle debut, but failed to build on it when pulled up next time; has since had wind surgery; hard to weigh up but the longer trip may help.
runners.comment: Pulled up latest but, if reproducing earlier promise, not without hope after wind surgery.

runners.name: Jolissaint
runners.spotlight: Would likely have made a winning start in an Irish point nearly two years ago but for a last-fence fall; unsurprisingly shaped as though in need of the run when 48l sixth on last month's hurdle/stable at Chepstow (extended 2m4f, soft) after a long absence; entitled to have come on from that and could be an interesting contender upped in trip.
runners.comment: Point winner; entitled to have come on from recent hurdle debut after a lengthy absence.

runners.name: Fountains Gala
runners.spotlight: From a good jumping pedigree but well beaten in her bumper and offered little encouragement again on last month's switch to hurdles; it's hard to make a case for her on what she's shown so far.
runners.comment: Good jumping pedigree but it's hard to make a case for her on what she's shown so far.

runners.name: Bits Ballou
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in a point bumper at Larkhill (good) in December and only hinted at ability when 40-1 for hurdle debut (2m, good) last month; needs to improve significantly for the longer trip.
runners.comment: Hinted at ability on last month's hurdle debut but needs to improve for the longer trip.

races.race_name: Newton Abbot Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Tobi Kanobee
runners.spotlight: Has failed to kick on sent handicapping, beating only one at Wincanton (2m4f) 22 days ago; needs to take a big step forward if he's to get off the mark at the seventh attempt.
runners.comment: Has failed to kick on sent h'capping; needs to take a big step forward to get off the mark

runners.name: Bredon Hill Dart
runners.spotlight: Course winner who was in good form until pulled up in 3m Chepstow handicap 19 days ago; he may have found that run coming too quickly so is worth another chance in refitted cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Course winner; in good form until pulled up at Chepstow latest; worth another chance
runners.owner: R W And Mrs Mitchell, D V Gardner

runners.name: Jetty May
runners.spotlight: Placed in points but this Jet Away mare has yet to offer much in five runs under rules, undergoing breathing surgery before coming in last of 11 on her first go in handicaps at Taunton (2m3f) last month; tongue tied now.
runners.comment: Placed in points but yet to offer much in five runs under Rules; tongue tied now

runners.name: Albert Park
runners.spotlight: Is 0-14 and he underwent a breathing operation before coming in only tenth of 12 in 2m4f Wincanton handicap 22 days ago; visor is reached for now.
runners.comment: Is 0-14 and only tenth of 12 in Wincanton handicap 22 days ago; visor is reached for now

runners.name: No Panic
runners.spotlight: Son of Nathaniel who was much improved on the back of wind surgery when fourth of 12 in 2m4f handicap at Wincanton 22 days ago; still lightly raced and weighted to go well (eased 1lb) over this longer trip.
runners.comment: Much improved after wind surgery when fourth at Wincanton 22 days ago; weighted to go well

runners.name: Green Asset
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden hurdler who wasn't disgraced when fifth of 11 in 2m6f Fontwell handicap in November; no forlorn hope off a 3lb lower mark after a break.
runners.comment: Not disgraced when fifth at Fontwell in November; no forlorn hope off a 3lb lower mark

runners.name: Masonbrook Meadow
runners.spotlight: Gained her breakthrough victory at Exeter (2m) in October but she's been off since coming in a below-par fourth in 2m4f handicap at Hereford in December; she needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Off since a below-par fourth in handicap at Hereford in December; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Keepkalmandkarryon
runners.spotlight: Lightly-raced 7yo who returned from 14 months off/wind surgery with a well-held tenth on his handicap debut at Southwell (2m4f) three weeks ago; has plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Back from 14 months off/wind surgery with well-held tenth at Southwell three weeks ago
runners.owner: DL Adams, JA Adams & G McPherson

runners.name: Gilded Glance
runners.spotlight: Little show in a trio of 2m maiden hurdles at Taunton (2) and Wincanton this year; this switch to handicaps needs to spark major progress.
runners.comment: Little show in trio of maiden hurdles this year; needs switch to h'caps to spark progress

runners.name: Irish Chorus
runners.spotlight: 2-4 since switched to fences, most recently against her own sex at Warwick (2m, good to soft) where the form has worked out well; steps back into open company off 6lb higher but she remains open to progress in this sphere.
runners.comment: Steps back into open company off 6lb higher but she remains open to progress over fences.

runners.name: Raffles Hermes
runners.spotlight: Twice a winner over fences in France before joining current stable; made a low-key start to British career but latest two efforts have been promising, latest when a keeping-on third at Fontwell (2m2f, soft) in March; return to better ground could see him in a better light and he's one to consider in an open contest.
runners.comment: Dual chase winner in France who is showing more of late and the better ground could suit.

runners.name: Siam Park
runners.spotlight: Four-time chase winner, most recently at Plumpton (2m1f, good) in November before falling in a Sandown Class 3 in January; returned from a break with two solid placed efforts in March over hurdles, latest when a close second at Taunton (2m3f, good) 21 days ago; switches back to fences in a very small field and should go well.
runners.comment: Leading contender back over fences after two solid placed efforts over hurdles last month.

runners.name: Dearkeithandkaty
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at Musselburgh (3m, good to soft) in his third handicap, before two creditable efforts when finding one too good; the step up trip could eke out further progress, especially if the cheekpieces prove a positive addition.
runners.comment: Nudged up 1lb but the step up in trip with cheekpieces added could eke out improvement.

runners.name: Welcheston Warrier
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden but, the Ffos Las blip in January aside, he's acquitted himself well in defeat this season and his recent form at staying distances has shown clear progress, taking another step forward at Hereford (3m2f, good) last time with the addition of cheekpieces, coping well with the quicker ground; leading contender.
runners.comment: Largely consistent and not yet fully exposed at staying distances; leading contender.

runners.name: I Love You Sivola
runners.spotlight: Has had just the one win, gained in a French chase back in 2022, and there's little in recent hurdle form to suggest he's about to add to that; 6lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Hard to recommend on recent hurdle form.

runners.name: Striking A Pose
runners.spotlight: Five-time hurdle winner who hasn't looked like adding to that on recent efforts, although latest was more encouraging over C&D; well treated on past form but needs to step up.
runners.comment: Offered a little more on latest start and well treated on past form, but needs to step up.
runners.owner: S Clare G Kennington & C Knowles

runners.name: Lightonthewing
runners.spotlight: Has more wins over fences, most recently at Exeter (3m, good to soft) last month under Ciaran O'Shea; failed to fire 20 days later but he's effective in this sphere as shown when a close second at Chepstow in February; yet to win from a mark this high but should be firmly in the mix.
runners.comment: Effective in this sphere but yet to win off a mark this high.

runners.name: My Own Lovely Lee
runners.spotlight: Has failed to build on early promise in maiden hurdles and made little impact switched to a handicap with cheekpieces added at Exeter (2m7f, good to soft) last month; more is needed to reverse the current trend and get his career back on track.
runners.comment: Made little impact switched to a handicap latest and more is needed if he's to figure.

races.race_name: Visit Kauto The King 6th May Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Sheldon
runners.spotlight: Infrequent winner, but running well since the turn of the year and his recent Newbury second (2m6f, good to soft) was solid; nudged up 1lb for that but he remains on a workable mark and is high on the list of dangers.
runners.comment: Infrequent winner but running well of late and remains on a workable mark; player.

runners.name: Soldierofthestorm
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdle winner who has taken well to fences this season, building on an encouraging second on chase debut when scoring from the front at Sandown (2m4f, soft) in March, jumping soundly throughout; this represents a different challenge but the 5lb rise is unlikely to halt his progress and another bold bid is on the cards.
runners.comment: Up 5lb but he's made an immediate impact over fences and can improve further.

runners.name: Hold Up La Colmine
runners.spotlight: Lacked fluency but created a good impression when winning at Windsor (2m4f, good) in November under Callum Pritchard; however, he's failed to kick on from that and returned form a break with a disappointing effort when beaten 33l at Fontwell (2m3f, soft) last month; return to better ground could help.
runners.comment: Hasn't progressed form a good start this season but a return to better ground may help.

runners.name: Tapley
runners.spotlight: Consistent 9yo who recorded his second win of the campaign at Stratford (extended 2m6f, good) last time, continuing a productive partnership with Isabelle Ryder; this drop back in trip shouldn't inconvenience and, despite a 3lb higher mark, he looks set to give another good account.
runners.comment: Consistent 9yo who looks sure to give his running again despite the 3lb rise; respected.

runners.name: Clotilda
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler who remains a maiden over fences after seven attempts, making the frame on all six completed attempts but without suggesting she's particularly well treated; back in trip but likely needs to find more off current mark to get her head in front.
runners.comment: Plenty of creditable efforts over fences this season without the win; likely needs better.

races.race_name: Newton Abbot Open Maiden National Hunt Flat Race (Category 3 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Max d'Airy
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on his bumper debut at Nancy for Noel George and Amanda Zetterholm in September; bought for 100,000euros after not discredited given he went freely (in a hood) when fifth in similar company at Kempton last month; remains with potential.
runners.comment: Debut second in France and not discredited when free-going Kempton fifth; can do better

runners.name: Taranis Dubh
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to 2m3f/2m4f hurdle winner Jack Hoola; dam, placed in a bumper/2m hurdle, is closely related to 2m5f/3m hurdle winner Moyross, out of 2m/2m3f hurdle winner from family of high-class hurdler Minorettes Girl; landed an Irish point in October so this son of Frontiersman must enter calculations for top stable.
runners.comment: Landed Irish point in October; son of Frontiersman must enter calculations for top stable

runners.name: Quickkick
runners.spotlight: Built on a debut fourth when third of 12 in Taunton bumper 47 days ago, finishing well having gone freely in a first-time hood; this son of Feel Like Dancing is capable of better still, especially if becoming more tractable.
runners.comment: Running-on third in Taunton bumper; capable of better still, especially if settling better

runners.name: Chocks Away
runners.spotlight: Getaway gelding who took a step forward from his debut when third of seven in Plumpton bumper last month; she may do better still so is not out of it with that form also having been franked.
runners.comment: Improved when third in Plumpton bumper latest; may do better still so not out of things
runners.owner: Wg Vestey Ag Vestey D Spencer Churchill

runners.name: Duhallow Storm
runners.spotlight: 100,000euros foal, 105,000euros 3yo; dam, useful bumper and 2m/2m1f hurdle winner, is a half-sister to useful 2m4f-3m hurdle/chase winner Me And Ben, out of unraced half-sister to high-class bumper and 1m7f-3m hurdle winner Rhinestone Cowboy; this son of Walk In The Park appeals on paper and rates an interesting newcomer for in-form yard.
runners.comment: 105,000euros 3yo; son of Walk In The Park rates an interesting newcomer for in-form yard

runners.name: Ta Ra For Now Jack
runners.spotlight: 66-1, last of eight in Chepstow bumper on her debut 52 days ago; significantly more is required from this daughter of Jack Hobbs.
runners.comment: Last of eight in Chepstow bumper on her debut; significantly more is required

runners.name: Gospel Moon
runners.spotlight: 100-1, beat one in bumper at Exeter on his debut in January; needs to take a major step forward.
runners.comment: Beat one in bumper at Exeter on his debut in January; needs to take a major step forward

runners.name: Jubilee Jack
runners.spotlight: Has failed to beat a rival in a pair of bumpers at Wincanton and Chepstow either side of Christmas.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival in bumpers at Wincanton and Chepstow either side of Christmas

runners.name: Speculateur
runners.spotlight: Point winner for Colin Bowe; fair debut for this yard in a bumper; consistent over hurdles without showing much improvement; twice placed at this venue and closely matched with Arcadian Emperor on running here in February; another solid run when third behind the two market leaders in a 2m4f maiden at Cork.
runners.comment: Consistent but no sign of progression, closely matched with Arcadian Emperor on C&D form

runners.name: Arcadian Emperor
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden point last season for Sam Curling; beaten favourite all three starts since then; possibly unsuited by a moderate gallop when favourite for a 2m4f bumper at Leopardstown in December; hurdling debut at Naas was encouraging, runner-up to smart bumper horse Shuttle Diplomacy; soundly beaten at this venue last time, but has a narrow edge over Speculateur.
runners.comment: Beaten favourite on all three racecourse starts, obvious chance but risky all the same

runners.name: Ballinaboola Hanna
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced when sixth at 100-1 over 2m here last time; longer trip may help
runners.comment: Not disgraced when sixth at 100-1 over 2m here last time, longer trip may help

runners.name: Eagle Doll
runners.spotlight: 11th foal; half-sister to winners, notably Mad Brian (hurdle/chase); plenty of stamina in the family.
runners.comment: Half-sister to winners notably Mad Brian (hurdle/chase), plenty of stamina in the family

runners.name: Darby Hill
runners.spotlight: Closely related to bumper/chase/hurdle winner St Cuthbert's Cave; wide-margin point winner for Padraig Butler at Kildorrery in February; promising recruit to Paul Nolan's team.
runners.comment: Wide-margin point winner for Padraig Butler at Kildorrery, promising recruit to Nolan yard

runners.name: Hi Ho Idaho
runners.spotlight: Placed in two points during the autumn campaign; encouraging display when third behind two Gordon Elliott-trained runners on hurdling debut at Leopardstown; place chance.
runners.comment: Placed in both starts in points, encouraging display on hurdling debut at Leopardstown

runners.name: Karlynn
runners.spotlight: Stepped up from debut when beaten 21l in fourth over 2m here last month; more needed.
runners.comment: Stepped up from debut when beaten 21l in fourth over 2m here last month, more needed

runners.name: Imtinkenofit
runners.spotlight: Second foal out of an unraced mare; nothing of much note in the pedigree; second reserve.
runners.comment: Second reserve, second foal out of an unraced mare, nothing of much note in the pedigree

runners.name: Caseys Cloud
runners.spotlight: Tenth foal; brother to 3m hurdle winner Harley's Pride, half-brother to three winners.
runners.comment: Brother to a 3m hurdle winner, half-brother to three winners, likely to need experience

runners.name: Me Wee Nanny Fran
runners.spotlight: Raced keenly on debut over 2m at this venue; may need more experience; first reserve.
runners.comment: First reserve, raced keenly on debut over 2m at this venue, may need more experience

runners.name: Little John
runners.spotlight: Dam bumper/hurdles winner, closely related to high-class winner Champ, out of half-sister to triple Cheltenham Gold Cup winner Best Mate; weak form in points, headgear tried now after a poor bumper run
runners.comment: Well-related gelding, weak form in points, headgear tried now after a poor bumper run

runners.name: Little Big John
runners.spotlight: Made a couple of mistakes when pulled up on his recent debut at Fairyhouse.
runners.comment: Made a couple of mistakes when pulled up on his recent debut at Fairyhouse

runners.name: Dancing Fruits
runners.spotlight: Very poor record in points; mild promise when a remote fifth on hurdling debut at Gowran; a lot more needed.
runners.comment: Very poor record in points, mild promise when a remote fifth on hurdling debut at Gowran

runners.name: Kome Dans Un Reve
runners.spotlight: Two poor runs last April; 200-1 chance when unseated early at this venue on return.
runners.comment: Two poor runs last April, 200-1 chance when unseated early at this venue on return

runners.name: Bruise Hill
runners.spotlight: Struggled in points and soundly beaten on his first two starts in maiden hurdles; will need to get a handicap mark; third reserve.
runners.comment: Third reserve, soundly beaten on his first two starts, will need to get a handicap mark

runners.name: Hungry Henry
runners.spotlight: Showed nothing in two maiden hurdle outings last season; can be ruled out.
runners.comment: Showed nothing in two maiden hurdle outings last season

runners.name: Solar Generation
runners.spotlight: Faded from two out on debut at Gowran last November; decent pedigree; needs to show major improvement.
runners.comment: Faded from two out on debut at Gowran last November, needs to show major improvement

runners.name: The Guinness Boys
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in a chase last time; handicap hurdle form is unconvincing, pulled up twice and not in the first eight in two completed starts.
runners.comment: Pulled up in a chase last time; handicap hurdle form is unconvincing

runners.name: Tangara Bay
runners.spotlight: Good second behind smart bumper performer Seo Linn on debut at Ballinrobe in August 2024; career punctuated by two lengthy absences; stayed on well to finish fourth on hurdles debut at Roscommon last summer; similar form when 14l fifth behind El Cairo in January; appeared to have plenty in hand when winning a maiden at this venue; strong contender.
runners.comment: Appeared to have plenty in hand when winning a maiden at this venue, strong contender

runners.name: Spellacy's Cross
runners.spotlight: Won consecutive handicap chases at this venue last October; was badly hampered when beaten 14l in fifh over hurdles here last month; beaten 20l in sixth at Down Royal on Sunday, 1 1/4l behind King Gris.
runners.comment: Won twice over fences at this venue last October, one place behind King Gris on Sunday
runners.owner: James M Barcoe & J F O'Neill

runners.name: Thisain'tthat
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far in sixth at Limerick on the most recent of three hurdle starts; open to improvement.
runners.comment: Not beaten far in sixth on the most recent of three hurdle starts, open to improvement

runners.name: Private Policy
runners.spotlight: Career has been punctuated by long breaks; coped better than most with very testing conditions when third of four finishers at Thurles (2m7f, heavy) last month on stable debut; two less encouraging runs since then; hard to assess, makes handicap debut.
runners.comment: Ran well on the first of three starts after a long layoff, hard to assess, handicap debut

runners.name: Strange Currency
runners.spotlight: Form of his third over extended 2m3f at Punchestown has worked out very well (second, fourth and fifth all won next time) but unplaced twice since then; cheekpieces on and and a good 5lb claimer booked.
runners.comment: Unplaced twice since a promising third in a maiden at Punchestown, good 5lb claimer

runners.name: Mic Drop
runners.spotlight: Point winner/course bumper winner in 2024; reverts to hurdling after two moderate runs over fences; others are more appealing
runners.comment: Point winner/course bumper winner in 2024, reverts from chasing, others are more appealing

runners.name: Paradise Lost
runners.spotlight: Flat winner; placed in three maiden hurdles early in his career; modest form overall in handicaps; Punchestown run in January was encouraging, not beaten far in fifth in a 16-runner event; fair third over 2m at Gowran last time, worth considering.
runners.comment: Flat winner, runner-up over hurdles three times, solid third over 2m at Gowran on latest

runners.name: Ned In The Park
runners.spotlight: Best maiden run when third at Punchestown last June; little impact on handicap debut at Navan, better at Gowran in August;  could go close if fitness holds up.
runners.comment: Shaped quite well when fourth at Gowran six months ago, could go close if fitness holds up

runners.name: Former Flame
runners.spotlight: Promise in three of four bumpers on good-good to yielding; another sound effort on good ground when a 10l second of 16 in a maiden hurdle at this venue last winter; absent since second in a maiden at Killarney last May; opening handicap mark gives her a fair chance.
runners.comment: Absent since second in a maiden at Killarney last May, fair mark for her first handicap

runners.name: Wakey Wakey Man
runners.spotlight: One of two for yard; 0-10 but has run well in defeat on a couple of occasions, including when a 5.25l third of 16 here in December (19.5f, heavy), shaping like further would suit; stayed on for an 11l fourth at Ludlow (2m5f, gd-sft) on latest off 10lb higher; interesting.
runners.comment: 0-10, caught the eye finishing well over 2m3f here in Dec, interesting at this trip

runners.name: Forceonmyown
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point and bumper winner; best effort in maidens a 5.75l fourth of 15 at Punchestown (2m, sft-hvy); weakened upped to 18.5f on handicap bow at Leopardstown latest; dropped 1lb but more needed up in trip; cheekpieces on.
runners.comment: Weakened over 18.5f on h'cap debut latest, cheekpeices go on and steps up in trip now

runners.name: My Gaffer
runners.spotlight: Two-time chase (including here) and hurdling winner has failed to exploit his lower hurdles mark on his last three starts; likes it here but remains 5lb above his last hurdles win and stamina to prove for this trip.
runners.comment: Course winner over fences, remains 5lb above last win, stamina to prove at this trip

runners.name: Rusty Harkness
runners.spotlight: Handicap debutant from top yard; definite promise in all three starts at 2m-2m7f on testing and good to yielding ground; could be on a workable mark; should go well.
runners.comment: H'cap debutant, promise in maidens at 2m-2m7f, opening mark may be workable

runners.name: Ballyglass Beauty
runners.spotlight: One of two for yard; last of three wins came at Listowel in 2023; fourth on both starts for this stable at this venue over 3m but beaten 31l (on heavy) and 52l (on yielding); on a dangerous mark now, however, and cheekpieces might provide a spark.
runners.comment: Remote fourths here last twice, on dangerous mark if cheekpieces provide spark

runners.name: Ontheropes
runners.spotlight: Has run well a couple of times this season but hasn't won under Rules' since October 2021; tailed off at Uttoxeter and pulled up in the Ulster National latest; needs to revive.
runners.comment: No win since Oct' 2021, tailed off and pulled up the last twice, needs to revive

runners.name: Cullig
runners.spotlight: Won off 84 on handicap debut at Cork (24.5f, good) in March 2025 and runner up on next two starts, off 97 and 100; progress has stalled, however, and pulled up in two of last three starts; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Progress of last season has stalled, pulled up two of last three starts, needs to rebound
runners.owner: Miss Patricia Coghlan & Diana Edwina Oakes & Guy Culliford

runners.name: Rhythmic Tune
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in good style at Limerick in October (2m5f, yielding); raised 7lb and well beaten on sole start since at Limerick but very testing ground a valid excuse; conditions here more suitable and could go well if ready after break.
runners.comment: Won off 7lb lower at Limerick on Oct', heavy ground an excuse latest, could bounce back

runners.name: Verdant Place
runners.spotlight: Has failed to fire over fences or hurdles this season; latest Cork fifth a remote one; trip and ground fine but needs to rediscover best; maybe the new tongue tie can help.
runners.comment: Yet to fire this season, latest fifth a remote one, maybe new tongue tie can help

runners.name: Better Times
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the second attempt in handicaps at Downpatrick last month (2m6f, yld-sft); 6lb higher now and this is harder but an extra furlong should pose no problems and there could be more to come, once there isn't too much rain.
runners.comment: Up 6lb for Downpatrick win latest, this harder but may be more to come once rain misses

runners.name: Ishan
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdles winner in 2023; has slipped 2lb below last winning mark and shaping though his turn might be near on recent starts at Limerick and Cork; rain would enhance his chance (all wins on soft and heavy).
runners.comment: Now 2lb below last win, shaped well the last twice, rain would enhance his chance

runners.name: Midnight Our Fred
runners.spotlight: Rated 19lb higher over fences but lost his form in that sphere; has failed to exploit his lower hurdles mark on last two runs over timber and may need quicker ground than this.
runners.comment: Failed to exploit lower hurdles mark last two starts in this sphere, best on good ground
runners.owner: Mrs Florence Lockwood & Emily B Lockwood

runners.name: Roxboro River
runners.spotlight: Won off this mark at Stratford last July (2m6f, gd-fm); beaten off 4lb higher at that venue when last seen in October; easy ground a concern on return from a break.
runners.comment: Won off this mark on fast ground last July, easy ground a worry here on return from break

runners.name: Rajsalad
runners.spotlight: No joy since a maiden hurdle win in February 2022; recent efforts have not indicated that a return to winning ways is imminent; cheekpieces now tried; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: No win since Feb' 2022, failed to complete four of last five, cheekpieces tried

runners.name: Getaway Charlie
runners.spotlight: On long losing run; pulled up on final start of last season and again on seasonal return at Fairyhouse in November; heavy defeat on his return from a 127-day break at Cork nine days ago; others appeal more.
runners.comment: On long losing run, poor efforts last thrice, tailed off latest, others appeal more

runners.name: No Big Deal
runners.spotlight: Deserved his C&D win in an amateur handicap last October after several solid displays; doubts raised by subsequent form but still merits respect in a race of this standard.
runners.comment: Consistent spell culminated with C&D win in December, decent chance in this grade

runners.name: Finnicky Filly
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in December; form has deteriorated since then; revival not out of the question, but has to be regarded as risky for betting purposes.
runners.comment: C&D winner in December, form has deteriorated since then, revival not out of the question

runners.name: Le Diablo
runners.spotlight: Mild promise in four starts for Pat Doyle; 33-1 when pulled up on handicap debut last October.
runners.comment: Mild promise when with Pat Doyle, 33-1 when pulled up on handicap debut last October

runners.name: Space Warrior
runners.spotlight: No success since a juvenile maiden hurdle win at Fairyhouse in November 2024; has dropped a long way in the ratings from an opening mark of 111 to 94.
runners.comment: No success since a juvenile maiden hurdle win, has dropped a long way in the ratings
runners.owner: Mrs Helen Mullins & M J Lomax

runners.name: Old Kenmare
runners.spotlight: Placed twice here last spring and would go close with a reproduction of his second 12 months ago; fell at the fourth at Cork on his first run after a lengthy break; may have needed the race here 12 days ago; should not be ruled out.
runners.comment: Second here a year ago, may have needed the race here 12 days ago, should not be ruled out

runners.name: Baltinglass Hill
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in a chase at Wexford last Friday; much more effective as a hurdler, though he he has added only one win to his tally since 2021.
runners.comment: Pulled up in a chase at Wexford last Friday, much more effective as a hurdler

runners.name: La Rocca Lass
runners.spotlight: Placed only once in seven bumper starts; tailed off at huge odds in four maiden hurdles; up against it.
runners.comment: Placed only once in seven bumper starts, tailed off at huge odds in four maiden hurdles

runners.name: Nukie's Artic
runners.spotlight: Poor form in maidens; in rear on handicap debut at this venue; tongue-tie will need to make a huge difference; top rider.
runners.comment: In rear on handicap debut, tongue-tie will need to make a huge difference, top rider

runners.name: Added Bonus
runners.spotlight: Veteran showed that he retains some ability when fourth in a 2m3f handicap here last month; however, that was his first time to make the frame since a Naas second in December 2024.
runners.comment: Veteran showed that he retains some ability when fourth in a 2m3f handicap here last month

runners.name: Lucky Crystal
runners.spotlight: Weak form in maidens; no better with a tongue-tie fitted on his recent handicap debut at this venue.
runners.comment: Weak form in maidens, no better with a tongue-tie fitted on his recent handicap debut

runners.name: Cock And Bull
runners.spotlight: Four-time Flat winner for Michael Bell, rated 78 at his peak; best form on good/good to firm and has been soundly beaten in three hurdle starts for this stable; potentially interesting if the ground is not too slow.
runners.comment: Four Flat wins in Britain, handicapped on winter-ground form, worth a market check
runners.owner: Sarah Dale & Wayne Dale & Ian Foster

runners.name: Katzoff
runners.spotlight: 0-18 over hurdles, placed once; unplaced in eight runs over fences
runners.comment: 0-18 over hurdles placed once, weak chase form, transformation needed on stable debut

runners.name: Marian Avenue
runners.spotlight: Huge odds for all his runs and has run accordingly; opening mark looks high but unwise to rule out; market may signal what is expected.
runners.comment: Huge odds for all his runs, has run accordingly, mark looks high but unwise to rule out

runners.name: Glendine Billy
runners.spotlight: 0-12 over hurdles; has a couple of placed efforts to his name but hard to fancy on early-season form; cheekpieces tried at Tramore last August are discarded
runners.comment: 0-12 over hurdles, twice placed but hard to fancy after a lengthy absence

runners.name: Ray Gun
runners.spotlight: Won at a modest level on AW in Britain; weak maiden hurdle form in three outings since December; better ground may help; top rider booked.
runners.comment: Won at a modest level on AW in Britain. weak maiden hurdle form, better ground may help

runners.name: O'Grady Cracker
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in three starts, including when 2-1 for an academy hurdle first time out; stable in excellent form lately; second reserve.
runners.comment: Second reserve, soundly beaten in three starts including when 2-1 for an academy hurdle

runners.name: Son Of Anarchy
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner; rated 130 over timber; got to within 1.75l of progressive Zanoosh on penultimate start in a handicap hurdle at Navan (2m4f, sft-hvy); bred for this game (dam a chase winner); this trip might be on the sharp side but can't dismiss.
runners.comment: Point winner, bred for this game, trip may be on sharp side but can't dismiss

runners.name: Pinot Gris
