runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; the best of these over hurdles (rated 143); no impact on first three starts for this yard in hot handicap hurdles; well beaten on sole previous chase start at Cork when with Gavin Cromwell but this much easier; wouldn't want it too soft.
runners.comment: Rated 143 over hurdles, this easier than sole previous chase start, rain would be a worry

runners.name: Nytol
runners.spotlight: Two-time winning hurdler (rated 119); always near the back in first two beginners' chases at Thurles (both 2m2f, soft); best form on good ground so needs the track to dry out.
runners.comment: 119-rated hurdler, soundly beaten in two beginners' at Thurles, needs track to dry out

runners.name: Mercury Mission
runners.spotlight: Hurdles winner (rated 113) unseated early when 50-1 on stable and chase debut at Downpatrick latest; prefers good ground and a clear round will likely be the aim here.
runners.comment: 113-rated hurdler, unseated early on chase and stable debut, clear round the aim here

runners.name: Charity Barnum
runners.spotlight: Eleven-race maiden under Rules; 0-9  over hurdles and rated 97; 125-1 and never involved when pulled up on chase debut at Thurles on latest; hard to make a case for.
runners.comment: 125-1 and pulled up on chase debut at Thurles latest, hard to make a case for

runners.name: Royal Hollow
runners.spotlight: Chase debutante is rated 129 over hurdles; can race keenly at times but likes cut in the ground and her yard had a Grade 1 winner at Fairyhouse last week; lacks a recent run but is one of the more likely ones here.
runners.comment: 129-rated hurdler, likes cut in the ground, yard in form, should go well on chase debut

runners.name: Beauforts Storm
runners.spotlight: 124-rated hurdler; bumper and maiden hurdle winner likes decent ground; 100-1 and detached when unseating at the third last in a hot beginners' at Thurles on chase debut; this less demanding but more needed.
runners.comment: 124-rated hurdler, unseated when 100-1 on chase debut, this easier but needs more

runners.name: The Goose's Acre
runners.spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles and rated 92; needs to improve massively for the switch to chasing; hard to fancy on all known form.
runners.comment: Chasing debutant, rated 92 over hurdles, has to improve massively, hard to fancy

runners.name: Goldinthemountains
runners.spotlight: Hasn't stood much racing since winning a bumper at Leopardstown in December 2023; remote sixth on chase debut at Punchestown in November and off since; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Remote sixth on chase debut at Punchestown in November, off since, others preferred

runners.name: Kamikaz Du Plessis
runners.spotlight: Rated 130 over hurdles but yet to replicate that level over fences; beaten a cumulative distance of 97l in first three chases at Fairyhouse (2m5f, yielding), Galway (22.5f, yielding) and Leopardstown (2m1f, yielding); best form on good ground so needs the track to dry out.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in first three chases, best form on good ground, needs track to dry out

runners.name: Great Universe
runners.spotlight: Course bumper winner; 1-6 over hurdles and rated 120; rated 7lb lower over fences after three chase runs; 7.5l behind Littlefoot at Downpatrick on latest and 10lb better off now so should get closer; place chance.
runners.comment: Course bumper winner, 7.5l behind Littlefoot latest, 10lb better off now, place chance

runners.name: Ad Caelum
runners.spotlight: Four-time winning hurdler; 0-10 over fences and yet to get to closer than 11l to the winner in this sphere; pulled up in Fairyhouse listed handicap latest; others have more appealing profiles.
runners.comment: 0-10 over fences, yet to get to closer than 11l to the winner in chases, others preferred

runners.name: Littlefoot
runners.spotlight: Relished the switch to fences when coming from the back to win on chasing debut in a Downpatrick beginners' (19.5f, yielding) just 16 days ago; this is a harder race but he did it nicely the last day and he should go well again.
runners.comment: Impressive win on chase debut at Downpatrick, this is harder but should go well again

runners.name: The Great Nudie
runners.spotlight: Won on her second chase start in a mares' beginners' at Naas (2m4f, soft); highly tried to no avail twice since, in a Grade 3 novice back at Naas and a listed mares' chase at Thurles; can't be discounted back in these calmer waters, especially if it rains.
runners.comment: Highly tried to no avail after Naas chase win, this easier, can't discount, likes it soft

runners.name: Coral River
runners.spotlight: Solid efforts in first three chase starts at Punchestown twice (16.5f, heavy) and last time when a close second at Fairyhouse (2m, soft); the third, fourth and fifth have won over hurdles and fences since; freshened up and big chance upped in trip (has placed form at 19.5f).
runners.comment: Solid efforts in first three chases, form of last run franked, big chance upped in trip

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares INH Flat Race

runners.name: Luciole Des Bordes
runners.spotlight: Solid fifth on debut in valuable Fairyhouse bumper a year ago; runner up on seasonal return at Punchestown (18.5f, yielding); turned over at short odds at Gowran latest (2m1f, heavy); step up in trip and better ground will suit here; worth giving one more chance to.
runners.comment: Runner up both starts this season, this longer trip should help, give another chance to

runners.name: Gortnaclea Castle
runners.spotlight: Fine effort on debut in a mares' bumper at Down Royal (17.5f, yielding) four weeks ago when a 1.75l second of 13 at odds of 12-1 (backed in from 66-1, two of these behind); uncertain whether this longer trip will suit but has a chance on form.
runners.comment: Fine debut effort when attracting market support, chance but longer trip a query
runners.owner: M O Quigley & Mrs H Lalor

runners.name: Tutu Twist
runners.spotlight: Placed in one of two point-to-points; 16l third on Rules' debut in a Naas maiden hurdle (2m, sft-hvy), staying on well; interesting upped in trip in first bumper.
runners.comment: Placed in point, stayed on well over 2m in Naas maiden hurdle, farther will suit

runners.name: Thelittleballerina
runners.spotlight: Ran a pleasing race to finish a 3.75l third on debut at Down Royal last month, weakening late on (2l behind Gortnaclea Castle); should stay this far on breeding and she ought to improve for that initial experience; each-way shout.
runners.comment: Pleasing run on debut at Down Royal, bred to stay and should improve, can't discount

runners.name: Giorgia Rose
runners.spotlight: Kapgarde mare showed ability without getting her head in front in five starts in point-to-points; off since last November and tongue-tied for Rules' debut; shrewd yard so check market.
runners.comment: Kapgarde mare 0-5 in points but showed ability, tongue tied for Rules' bow, check market

runners.name: Isdistheone
runners.spotlight: 0-6 in bumpers; kept on nicely for a 5.25l third at this track in November (16.5f, soft); below best last five starts and was beaten 25l when fifth in a maiden hurdle here last time; needs more.
runners.comment: Placed here over 2m in Nov', well below that level in five start since, needs more

runners.name: On A Diamond
runners.spotlight: Diamond Boy filly; half-sister to winners Butch (useful 2m4f-3m hurdle/chase), Eavesdropping (bumper and 2m3f/2m7f hurdle) and Hauraki Gulf (bumper and 2m5f/2m6f chase); dam unraced; makes a bit of appeal on paper so worth a market check.
runners.comment: Diamond Boy filly, half-sister to two bumper winners, worth market check on debut

runners.name: Myluckymaker
runners.spotlight: 80-1 when beaten 45l on debut at Fairyhouse; backed from 40-1 into 11-1 last time at Down Royal but raced keenly and finished in midfield behind a couple of these; probably capable of better.
runners.comment: Backed at big odds last time but failed to deliver, likely capable of better than that

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Prizeland
runners.spotlight: Oaks entry who has stamina on the dam's side of her pedigree; improved on an eye-catching Leicester debut last autumn when winning an ordinary fillies' novice at Kempton a few weeks later; potential for better again now tackling middle distances.
runners.comment: Won a fillies' novice at Kempton last autumn; has potential now tackling middle distances

runners.name: Keep It Cool
runners.spotlight: Not as good as Star Artist over hurdles; been offering more on the Flat, appreciating the step up to this trip when having Star Artist behind in Constitution Hill's Kempton race last time; handicaps will likely be her time to shine after this.
runners.comment: Appreciated the step up to 1m4f when having Star Artist behind last time; handicaps next

runners.name: La Tosca
runners.spotlight: Easy to back ahead of her 1m4f Tapeta debut late last month and only ran fairly in finishing fourth of six (third exposed as ordinary); receives weight all round and is entitled to improve, but needs to.
runners.comment: Easy to back ahead of her 1m4f Tapeta debut last month; entitled to improve, but needs to
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Star Artist
runners.spotlight: Fair hurdler who's finished well held in a couple of AW novice events this year, latterly behind Keep It Cool; this more realistic for a yard just getting going for the season but his immediate future probably lies in the hands of the handicapper.
runners.comment: This more realistic but his immediate future probably lies in the hands of the handicapper

runners.name: Okiru
runners.spotlight: Bumped into one on his handicap debut and stepped up on that a touch last time, when making most from stall 10 over C&D in first-time cheekpieces (behind Roccobear); better positioned today and is one to consider.
runners.comment: Made most from stall 10 over C&D last time; better positioned here and is one to consider

runners.name: Grand Echo
runners.spotlight: Exposed maiden who looked flattered from the front when going close at a big price at Kempton in February; back down to earth when well held last time.
runners.comment: Exposed maiden who came back down to earth when well held last time

runners.name: Militzie
runners.spotlight: Well below market expectations in a weak-looking Tapeta maiden early last month having shaped nicely on her 6f Polytrack debut; starts handicap life at a lowly level and the market should tell a tale.
runners.comment: Gone backwards from promising debut; starts handicap life at a lowly level; market useful

runners.name: Mighty Vega
runners.spotlight: Enjoying a consistent spell on the AW, including when winning over C&D late last month off a 3lb lower mark; looks set to give his running once more but is exposed now and vulnerable to improvers.
runners.comment: Looks set to give his running once more but is exposed now and vulnerable to improvers

runners.name: Starlight Sami
runners.spotlight: Gelded since finishing last of eight on his nursery debut (behind Mighty Vega) four months ago; stable enjoying a decent spring but he'll need much more from the outside stall.
runners.comment: Stable enjoying a decent spring; needs much more from the outside stall on comeback

runners.name: Roccobear
runners.spotlight: With his third yard already and offered much more for his new trainer when second at a huge price on his handicap debut over C&D recently; looked suited by the way that race panned out but it gave something to build on.
runners.comment: More for new trainer when second at a huge price on his handicap debut over C&D recently

runners.name: Sand Lily
runners.spotlight: Finished just ahead of Legacy Rock on her return from nine months off on Tapeta a month ago; may have needed that from a wide stall but faded badly late having enjoyed a good sit.
runners.comment: May have needed her comeback from a wide stall but faded late having enjoyed a good sit

runners.name: Legacy Rock
runners.spotlight: Big prices and well held in three runs last year before being gelded; favourite on his handicap debut at Wolverhampton last month, when never involved in a race in which the leaders didn't come back, and holds claims in a first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Favourite on his handicap debut last month; one to consider in a first-time tongue-tie

runners.name: Based
runners.spotlight: 65,000gns half-brother to two useful course winners; gelded since well held at big prices in three runs last backend; the market will be the best guide off a lowly opening mark.
runners.comment: 65,000gns half-brother to two useful course winners; the market will be the best guide

runners.name: Victory Ace
runners.spotlight: Picked up from Roger Varian for 40,000gns last autumn; absent and gelded since said to have finished stiff behind on his debut for this yard (among the winners lately) four months ago; the market will guide now handicapping.
runners.comment: Picked up from Roger Varian for 40,000gns last autumn; market will guide now handicapping

runners.name: Clipper
runners.spotlight: Inclined to pull hard last season, including down to 5f when last seen; needs to be more amenable from a wide stall if he's to have a chance.
runners.comment: Inclined to pull hard in sprints last year; needs to settle from a wide stall on comeback

races.race_name: Download The At The Races App Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Lara Antipova
runners.spotlight: Clear second on her sole start last summer (5.5f, turf) and again behind a stablemate of Lahan Kingman's on her Tapeta comeback in February; sets the standard on either effort.
runners.comment: Clear second on debut last summer and again on comeback; sets the standard on either run
runners.owner: Moulton Racing And Mrs A Jordan

runners.name: Leonie
runners.spotlight: Promise at a big price in a messy Wolverhampton maiden early last month but below that level when behind Lahan Kingman at the same course next time; drops in trip tonight and handicaps may be her chance to shine.
runners.comment: Behind Lahan Kingman latest; drops in trip tonight; handicaps may be her chance to shine

runners.name: Cosmic
runners.spotlight: 5,000gns foal, 50,000euros yearling who's the first offspring of a fair maiden (7f-1m) for Ed Walker; wouldn't need to be anything out of the ordinary in order to make a winning debut.
runners.comment: 50,000euros yearling; wouldn't need to be anything out of the ordinary on debut

runners.name: Lahan Kingman
runners.spotlight: Third behind a couple of subsequent winners on her Newbury debut last summer; held an Irish Guineas entry at the time of her Wolverhampton comeback four weeks ago, when a distant second (at odds-on) to her main market rival.
runners.comment: Distant second (at odds-on) to her main market rival on comeback; drops in trip

runners.name: Stolen
runners.spotlight: Related to a few winning sprinters for the Channon yard, notably Desperate Hero; well held on her sole start last summer but wasn't without support behind a couple of useful fillies; interesting to see how she goes in the market.
runners.comment: Not without support behind a couple of useful fillies on debut last summer; market helpful

runners.name: Lordsbridge Bay
runners.spotlight: Bit better with each run as he's gone up in trip; this half-brother to two course winners, including the yard's Lordsbridge Blu, starts handicap life at a lowly level and remains open to a bit of improvement.
runners.comment: Bit better with each run as he's gone up in trip; remains open to a bit of improvement

runners.name: Sail On Sailor
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to five winners who finished well held in four runs last year for Brett Johnson; not much expected on his Kempton comeback, so that was promising to run well in second place, and this longer trip should be fine on both that evidence and pedigree.
runners.comment: Promising return; longer trip should be fine on both that evidence and pedigree; player

runners.name: Dora Vita
runners.spotlight: Related to sprinters but worth a go over this trip on the evidence of her big-priced Wolverhampton handicap debut a month ago; had little chance the way that race unfolded and that gave some hope.
runners.comment: Little chance the way the race unfolded on handicap debut and that gave some hope

runners.name: Denby's Dream
runners.spotlight: Maiden who's done better since fitted with a hood round here; this a bit easier, dropped another 2lb back from a short break, but has stamina to prove upped from 6f (liable to be dropped in again from the outside stall).
runners.comment: Better since fitted with a hood round here; stamina to prove upped from 6f

runners.name: Lightning Hooves
runners.spotlight: Two poor efforts last autumn and another, having been gelded, on his Tapeta comeback a fortnight ago (said to have hung left); goes beyond 7f for the first time on his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Goes beyond 7f for the first time on his handicap debut; little to recommend him

runners.name: Parole Board
runners.spotlight: This is just her fifth start but she was always behind on her C&D handicap debut when last seen; makes little appeal in first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Always behind on her C&D handicap debut when last seen; makes little appeal

runners.name: Bosh Soldier
runners.spotlight: Beat a total of five home in as many starts for George Boughey and has continued to struggle for this yard; would be a surprise winner.
runners.comment: Beat five home in as many starts for George Boughey; continued to struggle for this yard

runners.name: Mohaab
runners.spotlight: Exposed maiden who's still to finish placed after eight starts; better off back into handicap company after four months off but is some way down the pecking order.
runners.comment: Exposed maiden who's still to finish placed after eight starts; others stronger

runners.name: Raspoutine
runners.spotlight: Down the field in four runs last summer; unlikely to come into his own until tackling middle-distances (half-brother to a Chester Cup winner among others) but the market is invariably the best guide with handicaps newcomers from this yard.
runners.comment: Unlikely to come into his own until tackling middle-distances; market the best guide

runners.name: Darkest Red
runners.spotlight: Said to have never travelled when among the favourites for a Kempton nursery that's produced winners, when last seen seven months ago; gelded since; market useful.
runners.comment: Said to have never travelled when last seen; since been gelded; market useful

runners.name: Adores
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to three winners, two of whom improved once handicapping in headgear; showed enough in three runs late last year, two of them over C&D, to warrant a market check at least.
runners.comment: Showed enough in three runs late last year, two over C&D, to warrant a market check

runners.name: My Old Mate
runners.spotlight: Issues at the stalls and inclined to pull hard in three runs late last summer (wore a hood down to 6f latterly); has since been gelded and begins handicap life, on his AW debut (dam a Listed winner at Dundalk), unexposed.
runners.comment: Been gelded; begins handicap life, on his AW debut (dam a winner round Dundalk), unexposed

runners.name: Mr Baloo
runners.spotlight: Won three last March, latterly off 8lb higher, and two the spring before; back in form when second to Helm Bay eight days ago and has every chance of turning the tables on 4lb better terms, going in first-time cheekpieces this evening.
runners.comment: Back in form when second to Helm Bay latest; every chance of turning the tables

runners.name: Criminal
runners.spotlight: Three poor efforts this year and the handicapper's proving slow to relent; this the shortest trip he's tackled, round an easy course (no problem with 1m4f last year), and he isn't obviously fancied from yet another wide stall.
runners.comment: Three poor efforts this year and the handicapper's proving slow to relent; outsider

runners.name: Epictetus
runners.spotlight: Not as good now, finishing some way off the front three when among the favourites at Doncaster last time; remains on a competitive mark, given a rare attempt without headgear, but has lost the winning habit (dry since his 3yo days).
runners.comment: On a competitive mark, given a rare go without headgear, but has lost the winning habit

runners.name: Warning Sign
runners.spotlight: Dual Sandown winner last summer (1m, 1m2f), latterly off 5lb lower, who stays a deal further; 66-1 when only having stablemate Good Speed behind on his C&D comeback last month and may need some help from the handicapper.
runners.comment: Dual Sandown winner last summer; may need some help from the handicapper

runners.name: Elouise's Prince
runners.spotlight: Serial hard-puller who scored for regular partner Jude Fernandes over C&D in February; will always come with risks attached but should get a good pace this time and the return here will help.
runners.comment: Serial hard-puller; will always come with risks attached but the return here will help

runners.name: Panama Black
runners.spotlight: Tapeta win early last year came off 5lb lower and he's looked to find this mark just beyond him this season; needs a career-best effort for his new yard, having been picked up from Brian Meehan recently for £12,500.
runners.comment: Needs a career-best effort, having been picked up from Brian Meehan recently for £12,500

runners.name: Good Speed
runners.spotlight: French winner in September 2024 who's started big prices and finished well held in three runs for his new yard, latterly when behind three of these and last of ten over C&D.
runners.comment: Well held in three runs for his new yard, latterly when behind three of these over C&D

runners.name: Arctician
runners.spotlight: Five-time Kempton winner who boasts a consistent record round here too; solid comeback from over three months off over C&D last month and is well drawn for one happiest on the sharp end but has never won off a mark this high.
runners.comment: Solid comeback from over three months off over C&D but has never won off a mark this high

runners.name: Helm Rock
runners.spotlight: Tended to do his winning in the past in batches, which bodes well under a 4lb penalty for last Monday's Kempton victory; well over two years snice he won off a mark this high and hasn't been at his best in two previous runs here but needs considering.
runners.comment: Over two years snice he won off a mark this high but needs considering for an in-form yard

runners.name: Big Sip
runners.spotlight: Went close first time out as a 2yo for Andrew Balding and landed the odds in a C&D maiden on his comeback last March, so has that in his favour; closed out last season with a win in a seller but will need to improve on his five handicap efforts.
runners.comment: Closed out last season with a win in a seller; has to improve on his five handicap efforts

runners.name: Newfangled
runners.spotlight: Decent record round here, including a C&D win last April, one of three victories in 2025; gone well fresh but is used to plying his trade in lesser company and resumes from six months off on a career-high mark (4lb higher than when last successful).
runners.comment: Decent record round here; resumes from six months off on a career-high mark

races.race_name: Free Tips Daily On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Bergamo Gold
runners.spotlight: Is enjoying a very good 2026 and bagged handicaps over C&D and at Wolverhampton (9.5f) in March; posted a solid third of seven in another 9.5f Wolverhampton handicap last time and she must enter calculations.
runners.comment: C&D winner and solid third at Wolverhampton last time; she must enter calculations

runners.name: Pangbourne
runners.spotlight: Improved for the switch to handicaps this term, running on into second at Wolverhampton (9.5f) 15 days ago; she's a player off the same mark with this slightly longer distance a positive.
runners.comment: Good second at Wolverhampton latest; a likely player off same mark over this longer trip

runners.name: Grey Sands
runners.spotlight: Blue Point colt who took a major step forward in first-time cheekpieces when impressively landing 1m Southwell handicap in January for Joseph O'Brien; changed hands for 26,000gns since and respected for his new yard despite an 11lb hike in the weights.
runners.comment: Impressive Southwell winner for Joseph O'Brien; up 11lb but respected for new yard

runners.name: Heart Sign
runners.spotlight: Failed to build on earlier promise when coming in last of nine on his handicap debut at Kempton (1m) 20 days ago; went freely there so has his stamina to prove over this longer trip.
runners.comment: Last of nine at Kempton latest; went freely there so has stamina to prove up in trip

runners.name: Gifts Differing
runners.spotlight: Has been brought along steadily, a patiently ridden fifth of eight in 1m maiden here 25 days ago; not out of things on her handicap debut with the longer trip a plus for this Teofilo filly.
runners.comment: Brought along steadily; not out of things on h'cap debut with her stamina unlocked more

runners.name: Chhota Saab
runners.spotlight: A fair sprint maiden at 2yrs but he's failed to fire this term, gelded before coming in last of ten in 6f Kempton handicap 13 days ago; steps up markedly in trip here.
runners.comment: Last of ten in 6f Kempton handicap 13 days ago; steps up markedly in trip here

runners.name: Lady Milton
runners.spotlight: Ended last season on the up with back-to-back 1m nursery wins at Kempton and Southwell in October; this A'Ali filly is a player if proving as effectiver over this longer trip.
runners.comment: Scored at Kempton/Southwell at 2yrs; this A'Ali filly is a player if effective up in trip

runners.name: Profit Street
runners.spotlight: In fine form in 2026, bagging a quickfire C&D hat-trick in March; ran another good race dropped to 6f here when third of 12 last time, bumped start; a player once more back at his ideal trip.
runners.comment: Bagged C&D hat-trick in March and a good 6f third latest; player back at optimum trip

runners.name: Bella Bisbee
runners.spotlight: Ended a run of modest effort when second of 12 over C&D 11 days ago; she needs to back it up now.
runners.comment: Ended a run of modest effort when C&D second 11 days ago; she needs to back it up now

runners.name: Twirler
runners.spotlight: Scored at Kempton (7f) in February but she saw her run of good form come to an end when seventh of ten over C&D 25 days ago; she's the sort to bounce back though.
runners.comment: Run of good form ended when C&D seventh 25 days ago; she's the sort to bounce back though

runners.name: Bold Suitor
runners.spotlight: Bagged AW handicaps at Wolverhampton (7f) and Kempton (1m) last summer; not disgraced after five months off when a fading fourth of seven at Kempton (7f) last month so possibilities eased 1lb.
runners.comment: Not disgraced after five months off when fourth at Kempton last month; possibilities

runners.name: Edergole's Gift
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who wasn't disgraced when third of seven to Profit Street over C&D 45 days ago; not discounted off an easing mark.
runners.comment: C&D winner; not disgraced when third over C&D 45 days ago; not discounted off easing mark

runners.name: Daisy Roots
runners.spotlight: Resumed winning ways over 1m here in September and she signed off for 2025 with a good C&D second the following month; has gone well fresh too so she needs considering on her seasonal return.
runners.comment: Signed off for 2025 with a good C&D second in October; needs considering on her return

runners.name: Chifa
runners.spotlight: Kickstarted 2026 with success at Wolverhampton (7f) in January and got back on track when seventh of 12 in another 7f handicap there two weeks ago given he was impeded early in the straight.
runners.comment: Got back on track when seventh at Wolverhampton two weeks ago; in the picture

runners.name: Zubaru
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who posted a solid fifth (in first-time visor) in 6f Windsor handicap in September; off since and needs to hit the ground running on his Polytrack debut.
runners.comment: Solid fifth at Windsor in September; needs to hit the ground running on Polytrack debut

runners.name: Victory Sound
runners.spotlight: A fair 7f winner for Charlie Hills in 2024 but he's offered very little for his current yard, beating just one in 7f Doncaster handicap on reappearance 16 days ago.
runners.comment: Beat one in 7f Doncaster handicap on reappearance 16 days ago; hard to warm to

runners.name: Midnight Ravens
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo; tried blinkered and tongue tied when last of eight in 1m Southwell handicap in October on his final run for Paul Attwater; lots more is required for his new yard.
runners.comment: Last of eight at Southwell in October on final run for Paul Attwater; lots more required

runners.name: Majestic Wave
runners.spotlight: Winless for his current yard and he ended last term under a cloud; needs to take a big step forward on his reappearance.
runners.comment: Winless for his current yard and he ended last term under a cloud; big step forward needed

runners.name: Kimi Rey
runners.spotlight: Not always consistent at two while showing very useful form, but that did include a Listed win at Deauville; recorded a clear career-best when beating a few of these recently in a C&D Listed event on heavy, and capable of better for her leading trainer.
runners.comment: Beat a few of these in a recent C&D event, making her one to beat on that effort

runners.name: Street Show
runners.spotlight: Doesn't lack for experience, with his final run as a 2yo (second in a course Group 2) and his 3yo return effort showing he is a smart performer; only beaten a head by Kimi Rey on that recent start, so holds sound claims.
runners.comment: Beaten just a head on his recent return to action, so holds sound claims

runners.name: Pearl Fortune
runners.spotlight: Wide-margin debut winner who didn't massively step up from that initially, but ended season with a fourth in a 5f Listed event at Ayr before winning a 5.5f Listed race in October at Deauville (very soft) from Jeudixx under this rider, with Kimi Rey third; place claims at least if returning from a winter off in that form.
runners.comment: Good chance of being involved if running to the form of her last run in 2025

runners.name: Afjan
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a solid juvenile campaign following a debut success, notably winning a 5f Group 3 at Longchamp (very soft) before running respectably in the Prix de l'Abbaye; has found Kimi Rey too good twice, though, so need more for the win.
runners.comment: Capable of smart form and good reappearance run, but more needed to win

runners.name: Soul Love
runners.spotlight: Won both starts to date, landing 6f events at Southwell and Newcastle, the most recent just over a month ago; interesting sort on turf debut for a trainer who has won this race twice in the past.
runners.comment: Interesting sort on turf debut for a trainer who has won this race twice in the past

runners.name: Kailani
runners.spotlight: Isn't going to fail for lack of race-fitness and was a three-time winner on the AW at Deauville over the winter at up to 7f, but was fourth, albeit not beaten far, in a race won by Kimi Rey recently that contained a few of these, so doesn't have the scope some have for improvement.
runners.comment: Not beaten far in a race won by Kimi Rey recently, but needs extra to do better

runners.name: Kathy Style
runners.spotlight: Stablemate of Kathy Light, who has a similar profile to that filly, winning before a respectable comeback this year; again hard to know what to make of the form and suspicion is she'll find this a tough test.
runners.comment: Profile similar to stablemate Kathy Light, and difficult to assess on Italian form

runners.name: My Calyx Cen
runners.spotlight: Progressive colt who is back to turf trying for a four-timer; one of his recent wins came on the AW at Pau over a mile, the other over 6.5f, so much depends if he can handle a further drop in trip; not discounted.
runners.comment: Back on turf and down in trip seeking a four-timer; interesting

runners.name: Jeudixx
runners.spotlight: Ran with credit plenty of times as a 2yo, but often behind something she faces here again today and needs a step forward from her recent effort over C&D; others easier to like for the win.
runners.comment: Behind a few of these last time, so needs improvement to get past them
runners.owner: Stars Of Fortune, Racing Boileau Et Al

runners.name: Yaupon De Replay
runners.spotlight: Started career fantastically when a 20-1 winner of Listed Blenheim Stakes at Fairyhouse (6f, yielding), but nowhere near as good when upped to 7f for the Group 3 Killavullan Stakes; no great surprise that she's down in trip for her 3yo return and stable is in good form.
runners.comment: Only had two runs as a juvenile, winning the first of them; stable in good form

runners.name: Kathy Light
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f good-ground maiden at Capannelle last November and ran with credit in a conditions event at San Siro 16 days ago, but will need more to be involved in the finish here.
runners.comment: Hard to be certain of how strong her form is but ran well in Italy on her return

runners.name: Argentine Tango
runners.spotlight: Ran 12 times as a 2yo, and would help set a good standard if running to her best of the summer in Group races, but clearly has less scope for more compared to some of these; trip no issue and seems best suited by a sound surface.
runners.comment: Helps to set a good standard on her best of last summer, but ran 12 times as a 2yo

runners.name: Moojeed
runners.spotlight: Progressed on first three starts and shed his maiden status in a 6f Group 3 here (good to soft) last year but couldn't follow that up in a Group 2 after; tongue-tie goes on for seasonal return and should give a good account.
runners.comment: Shed his maiden status in a 6f Group 3 last year; should give a good account

runners.name: Kimi Rey
runners.spotlight: Not always consistent at two while showing very useful form, but that did include a Listed win at Deauville; recorded a clear career-best when beating a few of these recently in a C&D Listed event on heavy, and capable of better for her leading trainer.
